title: High-Density Candlestick with the Canvas Renderer

files.name: chart.js
index.html
files.code:
The candle bodies and wicks are painted into a single <canvas> while the axes, grid, crosshair, zoom selection, and the OHLC tooltip stay as SVG. Hover to read Open/High/Low/Close, and drag to zoom, exactly as in SVG mode: the y-axis auto-scales to the visible price range on every zoom (via zoom.autoScaleYaxis: true). Note that with renderer: 'auto' candlesticks only promote to canvas at 50,000 candles (they have no per-point markers or data labels for the threshold to count), so this demo selects canvas explicitly. Switch to svg at 50,000+ candles to feel the difference.

Enable LTTB downsampling turns on chart.dataReducer with a threshold of 11,000 points, so the 2,000 and 10,000 counts always render raw while 50,000 and 100,000 aggregate to a tenth of their size (5,000 and 10,000 on-screen candles). Each bucket keeps its opening open, highest high, lowest low and final close, and every zoom re-samples just the visible window, so full per-candle detail returns as you zoom in. Uncheck it to compare raw rendering at every size: the stats line shows how many candles are actually drawn.

files.name: styles.css
